summary: Matched three small Kirby collision callback wrapper functions in ftKb_Init.c and added the required helper callback prototypes to ftKb_Init.h. The functions replace placeholder nonmatching markers for Kirby neutral-special collision states with direct calls into existing fighter collision helpers, passing state-specific continuation callbacks.
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_Coll as a direct wrapper calling ft_8008403C(gobj, fn_800F6588).
Implemented ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_1_Coll as a direct wrapper calling ft_80082C74(gobj, fn_80109C74).
Implemented ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_0_Coll as a direct wrapper calling ft_80082C74(gobj, fn_80109CF0).
Added prototypes for fn_800F6588, fn_80109C74, and fn_80109CF0 in ftKb_Init.h near related address-ordered Kirby callback declarations.
Replaced three placeholder decomp markers in ftKb_Init.c with matching C implementations.
smart_moves: Recognized these collision functions were simple state wrappers around shared fighter transition/collision helpers rather than unique logic.
Kept the implementations minimal: each function forwards the Fighter_GObj pointer and the appropriate callback to an existing helper, which is the likely matching shape for these easy_funcs.
Preserved address-ordered header organization by inserting callback prototypes beside nearby Kirby SpecialN and Yoshi-copy SpecialN callbacks.
Used existing unknown callback naming style, fn_XXXXXXXX, for helper callbacks whose semantic names were not established in the provided evidence.
decomp_lessons: For fighter collision-state functions, check whether the body is only a call to a shared helper such as ft_8008403C or ft_80082C74 with a continuation callback.

Small placeholder functions can often be matched by comparing neighboring collision functions in the same character file; ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_1_Coll already used the same pattern with ft_8008403C(gobj, fn_80109BF8).
When adding a matched wrapper that references an undeclared internal callback, add the prototype to the relevant character init header in address order.
Do not over-name unknown callbacks without evidence; this PR retained fn_800F6588, fn_80109C74, and fn_80109CF0.
naming_conventions: Matched Kirby action-state collision functions use the ftKb_*_Coll naming pattern.
Unknown or not-yet-semantically-named callbacks remain in fn_XXXXXXXX format.
Header declarations include address comments such as /* 0F6588 */ and are grouped in ascending address order.
Kirby Yoshi-copy neutral-special symbols use the YsSpecialN / YsSpecialAirN naming pattern.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a direct tail/body wrapper shape for easy functions: void function(Fighter_GObj* gobj) { helper(gobj, callback); }.
Match collision wrappers by selecting the correct shared helper: ft_8008403C for ftKb_SpecialN_Coll and ft_80082C74 for the two ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_*_Coll functions.
Add missing callback prototypes before compiling/matching so the generated call signatures stay consistent.
Neighboring implemented functions can reveal the required helper/callback pattern for nearby placeholders.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to the metadata, but its body was not included in the supplied context, so no comment-derived feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining Kirby /// #*_Coll placeholders that are likely direct wrappers around ft_8008403C or ft_80082C74.
Compare nearby ftKb SpecialN and copied-character neutral-special collision functions for repeated helper/callback wrapper patterns.
Look up the omitted issue comment for PR 1874 if reviewer or CI context is needed.
Investigate whether fn_800F6588, fn_80109C74, and fn_80109CF0 can later receive semantic names once their behavior is decompiled.
